articles.title: Salarios: tras la caída de febrero, se agranda la brecha entre sectores y persisten las dudas sobre una recuperación
articles.content: El salario promedio del Sistema Integrado Previsional Argentino (SIPA) mostró una baja en febrero, aunque en la gestión de Milei superan levemente a la inflación. Sin embargo, hacia el interior del indicador, los haberes de convenio -que alcanzan a millones de trabajadores- acumulan una baja real de 7 puntos porcentuales desde noviembre de 2023.

articles.title: Fed: Kevin Warsh prometió independencia de Donald Trump, propuso un giro en la comunicación y no dio señales claras sobre tasas
articles.content: Los analistas del mercado celebraron las declaraciones sobre la autonomía del banco central de EEUU, aunque reconocieron la falta de detalles en sus declaraciones.

articles.title: Las expectativas de los industriales no mejoran: menos del 4% prevé aumentar el empleo de acá a mitad de año
articles.content: De acuerdo con una encuesta publicada por el INDEC, la demanda insuficiente y la competencia de productos importados siguen siendo los principales problemas del sector.

articles.title: La confianza de los empresarios supermercadistas cayó a mínimos en seis meses: acusan problemas de demanda y financiamiento
articles.content: Según los últimos datos del INDEC, el indicador (ICE) retrocedió un 6,2%, y regresó a los valores críticos que se veían en septiembre del año pasado.

articles.title: EEUU: ventas minoristas subieron un 1,7% en marzo, mejor de lo previsto
articles.content: Es en comparación con febrero, cuya cifra se revisó al alza. La cifra de ventas minoristas subyacentes -excluyendo materiales de construcción, vehículos y piezas de vehículos, gasolineras y servicios de alimentación- subió un 0,7% en términos intermensuales, frente al aumento del 0,2% que esperaban los analistas.

articles.title: Beneficios de Cuenta DNI en abril: todos los ahorros posibles y las nuevas marcas que se adhirieron
articles.content: Conocé todas las opciones que tenés con la aplicación del Banco Provincia para ahorrar en lo que queda del mes.

articles.title: Lo que podés ganar en un plazo fijo de $500.000 a 30 días
articles.content: Este tipo de inversión sigue siendo una de las opciones más elegidas por los argentinos para invertir sus ahorros: conocé cuánto podés ganar.

articles.title: Cuánto sale un simulador de Fórmula 1: precios y alternativas para correr como Colapinto
articles.content: Uno de los dispositivos más divertidos y a la vez más completos para vivir una experiencia similar a la del corredor.

articles.title: Créditos hipotecarios: se reactivaron las consultas tras la baja de tasas, aunque el acceso sigue limitado
articles.content: Los bancos recortaron tasas desde marzo y varias líneas volvieron a ubicarse en niveles de un dígito. El apetito del público repuntó, pero los altos ingresos exigidos y los filtros crediticios aún frenan la recuperación del mercado.

articles.title: ¿Conviene invertir en plazo fijo UVA? El dato de inflación que reavivó el interés por la cobertura
articles.content: Tras la aceleración de precios al nivel más alto en un año, los depósitos ajustados por inflación vuelven a ganar atractivo frente a la tasa fija tradicional.

articles.title: How Mideast War Is Affecting SE Asia Investment Flows
articles.content: CGS International Group CEO Carol Fong says investors are pausing risk‑taking amid the Middle East conflict, but capital continues to flow into Southeast Asia, with Singapore seeing the strongest inflows. Speaking to Bloomberg, Fong also discussed the firm’s new private equity vehicle targeting opportunities along the China‑ASEAN corridor. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Small Caps Lead as India’s Stock-Market Rally Widens
articles.content: Nifty 50’s breadth signals a new bull market, according to Macquarie.

articles.title: China’s ‘National Team’ Cut ETF Stakes Below 20% Disclosure Mark
articles.content: China’s “national team” has stepped back from its dominant role in the country’s biggest stock ETFs, pointing to efforts to rein in an overheated rally earlier this year.

articles.title: Trump Is Being Pushed by Markets to End Iran War: Natixis CIB
articles.content: Alicia Garcia Herrero of Natixis CIB says "the markets want to push Trump and Trump will have to concede" when it comes to the duration of the Iran war. She tells Bloomberg Television that "anything that goes beyond two months, and we're very close to that, is going to be extremely disruptive."  (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Treasury Wine Jumps Most in 12 Years on Strong China Results
articles.content: Shares in Treasury Wine Estates Ltd. rose the most in 12 years after the Australian vintner announced a sharp jump in the amount of product shifting to retailers in key markets.

articles.title: Trump Keeps Blockade as Talks Falter
articles.content: President Donald Trump announced he was extending a ceasefire with Iran indefinitely a day before it was set to expire, even as plans for a fresh round of talks between the two countries fell apart. Bloomberg's Joumanna Bercetche has the latest. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Gold Recovers After Two-Day Drop as Trump Extends Iran Ceasefire
articles.content: Gold regained some ground after a two-day decline, as US President Donald Trump extended a ceasefire with Iran and allowed more time to arrange a fresh round of peace talks.

articles.title: Memory Stock Valuations Spark Debate Over ‘Supercycle’ Potential
articles.content: Memory makers are riding surging demand to record profits, yet their stocks are still trading at a fraction of the valuation multiples of other top artificial intelligence chip names.

articles.title: BHP Strikes Iron Ore Deal With China After Months of Talks
articles.content: BHP Group has struck a supply deal with China’s state-backed iron ore buyer, ending a months-long standoff between a vital consumer and the world’s largest miner that has roiled markets and hampered steel mills’ access to key products.

articles.title: US Stock Futures Rise, Dollar Falls on Iran Truce: Markets Wrap
articles.content: US equity-index futures climbed and the dollar edged lower after President Donald Trump said he would extend the ceasefire with Iran, spurring cautious optimism among investors.

articles.title: Trump extends Iran ceasefire as peace talks hit impasse
articles.content: President’s latest mixed signal comes as US sends more troops to region

articles.title: Why America is falling out of love with Israel
articles.content: Public opinion and elite sentiment has turned decisively against Netanyahu

articles.content: The outgoing North Carolina senator is refusing to back down in fight over president’s probe into Jay Powell

articles.content: Elon Musk’s rocket and AI conglomerate is seeking to catch up to rivals OpenAI and Anthropic

articles.title: Anthropic investigating unauthorised access of powerful Mythos AI model
articles.content: Start-up has limited the release of the new tool because of concerns about its hacking abilities

articles.title: Traders Push Korea Leveraged Bets to Record as Chip Stocks Rally
articles.content: Leveraged bets on South Korean equities jumped to a record as the return of the artificial intelligence trade prompts retail investors to chase a rally in tech heavyweights.

articles.title: Vietnam Taxi Firm GSM Expects to Hold IPO in 2028, Founder Says
articles.content: Vietnam’s ride-hailing taxi Green & Smart Mobility JSC, which is owned by the country’s richest man, plans an initial public offering in the second half of 2028, according to founder Pham Nhat Vuong.

articles.title: Oil Holds Advance as Trump Extends Truce But Maintains Blockade
articles.content: President Trump says the US would not launch fresh attacks on Iran but will keep blocking ships linked to the Islamic Republic until “discussions are concluded, one way or the other.” Bloomberg's Stephen Stapczynski has more. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: S&P 500 Futures Rise, Dollar Dips on Iran Truce: Markets Wrap
articles.content: US equity-index futures climbed and the dollar edged lower after President Donald Trump said he would extend the ceasefire with Iran, spurring cautious optimism among investors.

articles.title: Iran accuses US of violating international law over vessel seizure
articles.content: Iran’s ambassador to UN also accused Washington of ‘continuing internationally wrongful acts’ that breached ceasefire agreement

articles.title: Segantii Wins Permission to Use BofA Documents for Legal Defense
articles.content: Segantii Capital Management won a small victory ahead of its insider-trading trial, when a Hong Kong judge said the firm would be permitted to use key documents from Bank of America Corp. as part of its legal defense — something the Wall Street bank had tried to block.

articles.title: Iran Tankers Go Dark to Sail Past US Blockade Laden With Crude
articles.content: At least two fully laden Iranian tankers have sailed out of the Persian Gulf and past a US blockade this week, part of a flotilla that has made its way around the warships and ferried roughly 9 million barrels of oil to the market.

articles.title: Korea FX Deposits Slide by Record as Weak Won Spurs Conversions
articles.content: South Korea’s foreign-currency deposits fell by a record in March as a weaker won prompted companies to convert holdings into local currency.

articles.title: China’s Sale of Yuan Bonds in Hong Kong Draws Record-Low Yield
articles.content: China’s biggest yuan bond sale in Hong Kong since 2023 drew record-low yields for both two- and 15-year debt, as Beijing speeds up its push to internationalize the currency and mop up pockets of excess liquidity offshore.

articles.title: The TACO That Ate Market Strategy
articles.content: Why ‘buy the dip’ is Trump Always Chickens Out’s best friend.

articles.title: Markets Weigh Iran Truce as Risks Persist | The Asia Trade 4/22/2026
articles.content: "Bloomberg: The Asia Trade" brings you everything you need to know to get ahead as the trading day begins in Asia. Bloomberg TV is live from Tokyo and Sydney with Shery Ahn and Haidi Stroud-Watts, getting insight and analysis from newsmakers and industry leaders on the biggest stories shaping global markets. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Vietnam’s GSM Taxi Plans to Hold IPO in 2028, Founder Says
articles.content: Vietnam’s ride-hailing taxi Green & Smart Mobility JSC, which is owned by the country’s richest man, plans an initial public offering in the second half of 2028, according to founder Pham Nhat Vuong.

articles.title: Six lessons from history’s greatest financial crises
articles.content: The Babylonians had debt defaults. The S&L scandal led to 2008. What else does the past tell us?

articles.title: Pennsylvania’s chipmaking comeback left in limbo under Donald Trump
articles.content: High-tech semiconductor manufacturing began in the Lehigh Valley but promised federal funds for its revival have not come through

articles.title: The real transatlantic divide is about more than Trump
articles.content: Europe and the US have fundamentally different world views when it comes to risk, force and international law

articles.title: How Nestlé’s pioneering China business fell into disarray
articles.content: Chinese distributors are chasing the Swiss giant for refunds after it allegedly oversupplied the market as demand dropped

articles.title: PBOC Injects Cash Despite Flush Liquidity, Fueling Bond Rally
articles.content: China’s central bank has injected more cash into the banking system, signaling unusual tolerance for abundant liquidity and boosting confidence that the bond rally may have further to run.

articles.title: UK Stocks Set to Fall, Pound Up Ahead of Inflation Data
ABB Raises Annual Revenue Outlook as Quarterly Orders Surge
articles.content: ABB Ltd. raised its revenue expectations for the year after a jump in orders related to data centers during the first quarter, driving demand for the company’s power-grid products.

articles.title: Iran Tankers Go Dark to Sail Past US Blockade Laden With Oil
articles.content: At least two fully laden Iranian tankers have sailed out of the Persian Gulf and past a US blockade this week, part of a flotilla that has made its way around the warships and ferried roughly 9 million barrels of oil to the market.

articles.title: Indonesia Bourse to Remove Tightly Held Firms From Some Indexes
articles.content: The Indonesia Stock Exchange plans to remove firms identified for having high shareholding concentration from some of its key indexes as part of ongoing market reforms.

articles.title: Oil Falls After Iran Says It Received Sign US Will End Blockade
articles.content: Oil fell after Iran said that it received “some sign” that the US was willing to end its blockade, opening the path for talks between the nations.

articles.title: Iran's Hormuz Toll 'Bluster': Signum's Bishop
articles.content: Andrew Bishop, Signum Global’s Head of Policy Research, describes Iranian attempts to impose a legal transit fee through the Strait of Hormuz as 'bluster', arguing Tehran is using the Strait as leverage to extract additional concessions from the US. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Oil Extends Drop, US Futures Rise on Iran Optimism: Markets Wrap
articles.content: US equity-index futures climbed and the dollar weakened after President Donald Trump said he would extend the ceasefire with Iran, spurring cautious optimism among investors.

articles.title: Convenience Store Operator Yesway Raises $280 Million in US IPO
articles.content: Convenience store operator Yesway Inc. raised $280 million in an upsized initial public offering priced at the bottom of the marketed range.

articles.title: Nordea First-Quarter Fee Growth Offsets Drop in Lending Income
articles.content: Finnish lender Nordea Bank Abp reported profit from lending in the first quarter that declined largely in line with estimates, counterbalanced by profit generated from fees.

articles.title: European Gas Steadies Amid Uncertainty Over Iran Peace Talks
articles.content: European natural gas steadied as uncertainty over the Iran war persisted, even as Washington’s truce extension spurred cautious optimism in some markets.

articles.title: Reckitt Sales Hit by Lackluster US Demand for Cold Medicines
articles.content: Reckitt Benckiser Group Plc reported weaker-than-expected sales on sluggish demand for its cold medicines in the US and as the conflict in the Middle East hit supplies in the region.

articles.title: Horizons Middle East & Africa 4/22/2026
articles.content: Horizons Middle East & Africa is your daily spotlight on one of the world's fastest-growing regions. Live from Dubai, we bring you the latest global markets and analysis, plus news-making interviews, with a special focus on MEA. All that and more, as you head to the office in the Gulf, pause for lunch in Hong Kong, or start your day in London or Johannesburg.  (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: China’s Oil Majors Sell Barrels as Run Rates Drop to 2022 Low
articles.content: Chinese oil majors are selling cargoes of West African and other crudes, according to traders familiar with the deals, a rare move that follows utilization cuts across government-owned refiners as the war in Iran upends global supply.

articles.title: Nordea, Handelsbanken Report Fee Growth as Lending Income Falls
articles.content: Nordic lenders Nordea Bank Abp and Svenska Handelsbanken AB reported profit from lending in the first quarter that declined largely in line with estimates, counterbalanced by profit generated from fees.

articles.title: US IPO Candidates Discouraged By Risk of Litigation, Red Tape
articles.content: The risk of being sued after an initial public offering is one of the biggest concerns for potential US listing candidates, according to more than 150 executives surveyed by Bloomberg Intelligence.

articles.title: Iran attacks container ship near Oman
articles.content: Strike follows Tehran’s ambassador to UN accusing US of ‘wrongful acts’ in breach of ceasefire agreement

articles.title: Military briefing: Trump bolsters Gulf force
articles.content: A third US carrier group and up to 10,000 elite troops expected in region by end of month

articles.title: ECB has ‘luxury’ to wait on interest rate rises, says top policymaker
articles.content: Latvian central bank governor Mārtiņš Kazāks sees no rush to respond to higher energy prices driven by Iran war

articles.title: Philip R. Lane: Expanding the supply of euro safe assets
ECB’s Lane Sees ‘Natural’ Fiscal Case for Europe Common Debt
articles.content: European Central Bank Chief Economist Philip Lane said there’s a logical fiscal case for the region’s nations to issue joint bonds, though such a move would need to be underpinned by trust.

articles.title: Earnings to Keep Driving Equities Higher, Schroders Says
articles.content: Patrick Brenner, CIO of multi-asset at Schroders, discusses earnings and equity markets. "Earnings have continued to deliver strongly," he tells Bloomberg Television. "As long as earnings are strong, we see no reason why equities should slow down," Brenner adds. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Norway Takes Over Planning for Rare Earth Mine From Municipality
articles.content: Norway’s government will take over the planning from local authorities for what is touted to be Europe’s largest documented occurrence of rare earth elements, vital for the electronic and defense industries.

articles.title: Indian Funds Buy 30-Year Bonds as Local Play Against Iran War
articles.content: Some Indian fund managers are buying longer-term government bonds, saying these are less sensitive to global volatility driven by the Iran conflict.

articles.title: Stocks Rise on Big Earnings Day as War Talks Stall: Markets Wrap
articles.content: Stocks rose after President Donald Trump indefinitely extended his ceasefire with Iran on a day when traders will also focus on a broad slate of corporate earnings.

articles.title: Nordea Traders Hit by Losses on Surging Interest Rate Outlook
articles.content: Nordea Bank Abp’s rates traders were wrong-footed in March when the Iran war caused a rapid change in the market outlook for monetary policies.

articles.title: Bank Indonesia Holds Key Rate, Vows Further FX Intervention
articles.content: Bank Indonesia held its benchmark interest rate unchanged for a seventh straight meeting, avoiding a reduction that could threaten the beleaguered rupiah or a hike that could threaten sentiment and growth.

articles.title: Deutsche Telekom Weighs Full Combination With T-Mobile
articles.content: Deutsche Telekom AG is considering a full combination with its American arm T-Mobile US Inc., a move that would create a multinational telecom group and rank as the largest-ever public M&A deal, people with knowledge of the matter said.

articles.title: Crypto Whale Moves 20-Strong Trading Team to Tap Asia’s Rich
articles.content: Crypto tycoon Li Lin is moving an entire trading team of about 20 people from his single-family office to Bitfire Group Holdings Ltd., a wealth manager in which he owns a 30% stake, to help the firm tap more private banking clients.

articles.title: Danone Sales Rise as Yogurt, Water Outweigh Formula Recalls
articles.content: Danone SA sales increased at the start of the year as consumers’ appetite for protein-rich yogurts and Evian bottled water outweighed the impact of an infant formula recall.

articles.title: Iran attacks container ship after Trump extends ceasefire
articles.content: US president extends truce after peace talks hit impasse

articles.title: Stocks Rise on Big Earnings Day as Brent Hits $100: Markets Wrap
articles.content: US stock futures rose after President Donald Trump indefinitely extended his ceasefire with Iran on a day when traders will also focus on a broad slate of corporate earnings.

articles.title: Australian Gas Exporter Santos Streamlines Business to Cut Costs
articles.content: Santos Ltd. is restructuring the company’s oil and gas business in order to cut costs, according to an internal notice seen by Bloomberg, after a string of stymied takeover bids in recent years that have raised pressure to increase shareholder returns.

articles.title: Increased Jet Fuel Costs Hit Global Airline Industry
articles.content: Airlines around the world are grounding more planes to cope with the increases in jet-fuel prices. Global capacity for May has been reduced by about 3 percentage points, with all but one of the 20 largest airlines slashing flights, according to data compiled by analytics firm Cirium Ltd. Lufthansa is scrubbing 20,000 uneconomic short-haul flights from its European summer schedule to save on fuel. Earlier in the week, United Airlines slashed its full-year profit forecast due to higher fuel prices caused by war in the Middle East. Bloomberg's Benedikt Kammel breaks down the situation. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Russia Caps Fertilizer Exports Till December in Global Crunch
articles.content: Russia extended fertilizer export quotas until December as a global deficit deepens due to the Iran war and disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz, a key route for the seaborne trade in nutrients.

articles.title: Ukraine Ready to Resume Druzhba Flows To Hungary, Slovakia
articles.content: Ukraine is ready to resume oil flows through the Druzhba pipeline, Hungarian refiner Mol Nyrt. said, a step that’s expected to unblock crucial European Union aid to Kyiv on Wednesday.

articles.title: China Wind Turbine Maker Eyes Spain for Factory After UK Snub
articles.content: Ming Yang Smart Energy Group Ltd. is considering building a wind turbine factory in Spain after the UK blocked the Chinese company’s plans for a facility in Scotland, citing risks to national security.

articles.title: Deutsche Telekom, T-Mobile Would Be World’s Biggest M&A Deal
articles.content: The technology, media and telecommunications sector has been behind the world’s largest public M&A deals and could be on course to deliver a fresh record.

articles.title: Microsoft to Face UK Class Action Over Cloud Computing Licencing
articles.content: Microsoft Corp. must face a UK class action trial over allegations it abused its dominant position in the market to overcharge businesses that used the Windows Server operating system and Azure, an antitrust tribunal has ruled.

articles.title: UK Inflation Accelerates on Higher Petrol Costs
articles.content: UK inflation accelerated in March, as a surge in energy costs triggered by the Iran war started to hit consumers in the pocket. The consumer prices index rose 3.3% from a year earlier, up from 3% the previous month, the Office for National Statistics said Wednesday. Santander Corporate and Investment Banking Economist Victoria Clarke reacts to the data on Bloomberg Television. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Two container ships attacked in Strait of Hormuz after Trump extends ceasefire
articles.content: US president extends truce after peace talks hit impasse

articles.title: Reckitt’s Sales Weighed Down by Cold Drugs, Iran War Fallout
articles.content: Reckitt Benckiser Group Plc reported weaker-than-expected sales on lackluster demand for its cold and flu medicines in the US and as the conflict in the Middle East hit supplies in the region.

articles.title: European Gas Resumes Advance Amid Uncertainty Over Iran Talks
articles.content: European natural gas resumed its advance as uncertainty over the duration of the supply disruption from the Iran war persisted, even as Washington extended a truce.

articles.title: Nordea Traders Hit by Losses on Surging Rates Expectations
articles.content: Nordea Bank Abp’s rates traders were wrong-footed in March when the Iran war caused a rapid change in the market outlook for monetary policies.

articles.title: Exxon Mobil Is Said to Consider Sale of Hong Kong Gas Stations
articles.content: Exxon Mobil Corp. is exploring a sale of its gasoline station network in Hong Kong, according to people familiar with the matter, as the US oil and gas giant continues to trim its retail footprint globally.

articles.title: Greece Announces Fresh  Relief Measures After Budget Outperforms
articles.content: Greece announced fresh economic relief measures worth around €500 million ($587.6 million), after data showed the country’s budget outperformed its targets last year.

articles.title: Oil Swings on Elusive Peace Deal Outlook, Iran Gunboat Attacks
articles.content: Oil swung between gains and losses as attempts to resolve the seven-week conflict between the US and Iran struggled, and Iranian gunboats opened fire on two vessels near the critical Strait of Hormuz waterway.

articles.title: EU Proposes Further Energy Measures to Mitigate Iran War Crisis
articles.content: The European Union proposed measures such as optimizing jet fuel distribution, as it unveiled a plan to navigate the energy crisis caused by the Iran war.

articles.title: Moscow to suspend Kazakh oil flows through key pipeline supplying Berlin

articles.title: Taxes on wages hit decade high across OECD countries
articles.content: Governments tap labour income as an ‘easy’ revenue raiser but risk undermining incentives to work and hire

articles.title: Frank Elderson: Interview with NRC
Traders Warn Iran Tolls Risk Setting Global Trade Precedent
articles.content: Shipping executives at two of the world’s biggest commodity traders said they’re concerned Iranian tolls on the Strait of Hormuz set a dangerous precedent for the free flow of global trade.

articles.title: The Hottest Stocks Going Are Among the Cheapest, for Good Reason
articles.content: Memory stocks sell at single-digit multiples on skepticism AI boom will last.

articles.title: Sandvik’s Orders Surge as Signs of Industrial Recovery Appear
articles.content: Sandvik AB’s orders grew the fastest since 2022 as it benefited from improving sentiment among industrial customers and continued demand for mining equipment.

articles.title: Traders Bet on Post-War Calm to Keep Rates in Tight Range
articles.content: Traders are building up bets that bond market volatility will keep falling, even as a US-Iran peace agreement remains elusive.

articles.title: World Cup Fans Face Transport Mess as NJ Clashes With FIFA
articles.content: Millions of fans gearing up for the 2026 World Cup have already been hit with sticker shock over ticket prices. Now many are realizing that getting to the stadiums will cost them even more. Michelle Kaske explains  (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Ferrexpo Slumps After Warning Cash May Run Out by End of August
articles.content: Ferrexpo Plc slumped 13% after the Ukrainian iron ore producer warned that without fresh funding it could run out of cash by the end of August.

articles.title: Germany Probably Eked Out Growth in Early 2026, Bundesbank Says
articles.content: Germany’s economy, Europe’s largest, probably expanded slightly in the first quarter though was weighed down by the Iran war and longstanding structural issues, according to the Bundesbank.

articles.title: Polymarket Loses Prediction-Market Lead After Delays, Blowback
articles.content: Polymarket, the long-time leader in prediction market trading volume, has fallen behind its chief rival as it faces a growing list of operational stumbles in its attempt to reach a crucial audience: US customers.

articles.title: Iran says it has seized two container ships in Strait of Hormuz
articles.content: Oil rises back above $100 a barrel following incidents in waters close to Iran

articles.title: Germany to take on ‘more responsibility’ for Europe’s defence
articles.content: Berlin publishes its first military strategy since second world war

articles.title: Dollar gives up bulk of war gains as traders abandon bullish bets
articles.content: Almost every major currency has gained against the greenback this month amid hopes for end to Iran conflict

articles.title: El dólar oficial ya acumula más de un mes debajo de $1.400 ante el aumento de la oferta de divisas
articles.content: La cotización mayorista retrocedió tras dos ruedas alcistas y continúa lejos del techo de la banda cambiaria. El mercado sigue favorecido por la liquidación del agro, emisiones privadas y menor demanda para atesoramiento.

articles.title: ARCA reconfigura el régimen aduanero para operaciones en plataformas marinas: punto por punto qué cambia para actividades offshore
articles.content: El organismo fijó nuevos procedimientos para registrar mercaderías destinadas a la exploración y explotación de recursos naturales y eliminó una normativa vigente desde 1996.

articles.title: Boom exportador primario y dólar calmo: analistas ven improbable una crisis del sector externo
articles.content: La debilidad del consumo interno y de la actividad industrial le asegura al Gobierno que no haya una demanda de dólares del sector productivo para insumos y bienes de capital, a diferencia de otros momentos.

articles.title: Efecto guerra: crece el interés global por ETF de energía nuclear
articles.content: Con cautela, pero a paso firme, tímidamente, gestoras globales apuestan a lanzar fondos ETFs de energía nuclear para posicionarse en una industria que ya prometía expandirse antes del inicio de la guerra en el Golfo Pérsico.

articles.title: Diálogos de Wall Street: EEUU y los mercados vislumbran un acuerdo para evitar guerra larga
articles.content: EEUU e Irán acordaron volver a reunirse para negociar en Pakistán. Esta vez sobre el filo del cese de fuego. ¿Qué cabe esperar, Gordon Gekko?

articles.title: Sin gastar un solo dólar: el destino imperdible del Mundial 2026 que es completamente gratuito
articles.content: Este evento abierto al público tiene música, comidas y partidos en vivo con acceso a una experiencia que muy pocos conocen.

articles.title: Boeing Narrows Cash Burn in First Quarter as Deliveries Rise
articles.content: Boeing Co. reported lower-than-expected cash outflow as it delivered the most aircraft in the first quarter since 2019, continuing its recovery with higher output and more steady operations at its defense and services units.

articles.title: EU Proposes Plan to Soften Energy Price Shock From Iran War
articles.content: The European Union proposed measures such as optimizing jet fuel distribution and cutting energy taxes to navigate the Iran war-driven crisis that it said could reverberate for years.

articles.title: US Premarket Movers for April 22, 2026
articles.content: S&P 500 Index futures rise 0.5% as of 7:50 a.m. in New York the morning after President Donald Trump extended a ceasefire with Iran.

articles.title: Oil Trader CFOs Say Hormuz Closure Driving Wave of Disputes
articles.content: The chief financial officers of some of the world’s top commodity trading houses warned that the protracted closure of the Strait of Hormuz may lead to a wave of disputes stemming from lost supply.

articles.title: VW Is Said to Shortlist EQT Group, CVC, Bain for Everllence
articles.content: Volkswagen AG has picked suitors for the next round of bidding for its heavy diesel engine unit Everllence, according to people familiar with the matter.

articles.title: Coffee Climbs on Iran Conflict Concerns and Brazil Weather Risk
articles.content: Arabica coffee climbed the most in two weeks in New York, amid concerns that the Middle East conflict will keep logistics costs elevated and as weather threatens Brazilian crops.

articles.title: ECB’s Stournaras Says Central Bank ‘Should Wait’ on Rate Hike
articles.content: European Central Bank Governing Council member Yannis Stournaras said the central lender “should wait” ahead of a rate decision meeting next week.

articles.title: Directors Quit Hotel Unit at Center of Singapore Billionaire Kwek Feud
articles.content: Three directors have abruptly resigned from a hotel subsidiary controlled by one of Singapore’s wealthiest families, the latest twist in a corporate shakeup triggered by an internal feud.

articles.title: Britain’s desperate need for Keir Badenoch
articles.content: The country requires centre-left foreign policy and centre-right reform at home

articles.title: Carlos Melconian cuestionó el plan económico de Javier Milei: "Si termina con 40% de inflación, es un fracaso"
articles.content: El economista advirtió sobre desequilibrios entre actividad e inflación, alertó por la caída del poder adquisitivo y sostuvo que los sectores más golpeados son comercio, industria y construcción.

articles.title: La inflación en Reino Unido subió al 3,3% y marcó su nivel más alto de 2026 por el impacto de los combustibles
articles.content: El encarecimiento de la energía tras la guerra en Medio Oriente impulsó los precios en marzo. El dato complica al Banco de Inglaterra, que enfrenta el dilema entre sostener tasas altas o asistir a una economía con señales de desaceleración.

articles.title: Boom exportador primario y dólar calmo: los analistas ven improbable una crisis del sector externo
articles.content: La debilidad del consumo interno y de la actividad industrial le asegura al Gobierno que no haya una demanda de dólares del sector productivo para insumos y bienes de capital, a diferencia de otros momentos.

articles.title: Optiver Taps Ex-Citadel Securities Trader to for Options Expansion
articles.content: Optiver has recruited Lawrence Tanzman, a former Citadel Securities trader, as the Dutch market maker expands into handling options trades sent by retail brokerages, a business long dominated by the Ken Griffin-owned firm.

articles.title: India’s Rate Setters Decided to Wait and Watch Conflict Impact
articles.content: India’s policy makers decided to pause in April to get a clearer picture of the shock caused by the war in the Middle East, minutes of the last meeting showed.

articles.title: ECB Officials Say April Is Too Early to Decide on Rate Hikes
articles.content: Two European Central Bank officials said it’s too soon to lift borrowing costs this month as the full consequences of the Iran conflict remain unclear.

articles.title: Loonie’s Weakening Ties to Oil Prices Amplifies Bets Against It
articles.content: When oil prices surge, the Canadian dollar usually strengthens — but that dynamic has shifted.

articles.title: Australian Gas Exporter Santos Streamlines Firm to Cut Costs
articles.content: Santos Ltd. is restructuring the company’s oil and gas business in order to cut costs, according to an internal notice seen by Bloomberg, after a string of stymied takeover bids in recent years that have raised pressure to increase shareholder returns.

articles.title: Oil Rises on Elusive Peace Deal Outlook, Iran Gunboat Attacks
articles.content: Oil inched higher as attempts to resolve the seven-week conflict between the US and Iran struggled, and Iranian gunboats opened fire on vessels near the critical Strait of Hormuz waterway.

articles.title: Vietnam, South Korea Ink Deals on Security, Nuclear Power Plants
articles.content: Vietnam and South Korea signed twelve agreements including on security, energy infrastructure and nuclear power plants, following talks between the two nations’ leaders in Hanoi.

articles.title: France Sticks to 2029 Deficit Goal as It Trims Growth Forecasts
articles.content: French President Emmanuel Macron’s government presented a deficit-reduction plan based on lower growth forecasts for the next three years, which would make it more challenging for the country to get a grip on public finances.

articles.title: BMO, Teck, TC Energy CEOs to Advise Carney on US Strategy on New Panel
articles.content: Prime Minister Mark Carney is turning to some of Canada’s most prominent business executives, including Bank of Montreal’s Darryl White, to give him advice on how to handle Canada’s economic relationship with the US.

articles.title: The military man trying to save US-Iran peace talks
articles.content: Pakistan’s fatigue-wearing strongman Asim Munir takes unorthodox approach to mediating between two arch-enemies

articles.title: Boeing regains delivery lead over Airbus as revenues jump
articles.content: US aircraft maker narrows losses and posts widest gap over European rival since 2018

articles.title: Plazo fijo por el piso: así operan los principales bancos hoy, miércoles 22 de abril
articles.content: Rendimientos bajos en pesos obligan a recalcular estrategias mientras los bancos ajustan tasas y los ahorristas buscan no perder frente a la inflación.

articles.title: La guerra en Medio Oriente encarece miles de productos derivados del petróleo
articles.content: Proveedores ya informan incrementos de más de 10% en los costos de varios productos que se fabrican con derivados del petróleo. Alcanza a juguetes y se estima que hasta 6.000 otros artículos.

articles.title: Descuentos en Carrefour hoy: qué promociones hay este miércoles 22 de abril
articles.content: La cadena activa reintegros y rebajas con bancos, billeteras y beneficios para clientes registrados.

articles.title: Rogers’ Earnings Matches Estimates as Sports Assets Drive Media Growth
articles.content: Rogers Communications Inc. matched analysts’ earnings estimates and raised its 2026 outlook for free cash flow as it cuts capital spending. The shares rose.

articles.title: Stocks Rise After Solid Earnings, Brent Hits $100: Markets Wrap
articles.content: A slew of strong corporate profits lifted stocks a day after President Donald Trump extended a ceasefire with Iran, though attempts to resolve the conflict struggled without any signs that the key Strait of Hormuz will reopen soon. Oil climbed.

articles.title: United CEO Won't Comment on Possible Merger With American Airlines
articles.content: Scott Kirby, the chief executive officer of United Airlines Holdings Inc., said he wouldn't comment when asked if his company would be buying American Airlines. Kirby did say he is focused on leveling the playing field with foreign airlines and building a "great global airline that all US citizens can be proud of." He speaks with Bloomberg's Lisa Abramowicz from the company headquarters in Chicago. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: US Stocks Advance on Trump Truce Extension and Earnings
articles.content: US stocks advanced on Wednesday as traders examined the state of play for the Middle East following President Donald Trump’s ceasefire extension with Iran and cheered corporate earnings.

articles.title: GE Vernova Shares Climb on Strong Orders, Power Demand Boom
articles.content: Shares of GE Vernova Inc. rose as much as 15.2% on Wednesday after the gas turbine and power grid equipment maker reported strong order growth amid rising electricity demand.

articles.title: United CEO Sidesteps Merger Topic, Says Some Assets of Interest
articles.content: United Airlines Holdings Inc. Chief Executive Officer Scott Kirby sidestepped questions about a potential merger with American Airlines Group Inc., while acknowledging that the current climate of high costs and struggling rivals might offer opportunities for deals.

articles.title: India to Purchase Fertilizer at Nearly Double Pre-War Price
articles.content: India, the world’s top importer of urea, has agreed to buy the nitrogen-based fertilizer at sharply higher prices than in a previous tender as the Middle East conflict disrupts supplies and pushes global benchmarks higher.

articles.title: Goldman Names Raman as Head of Alternatives Capital Markets
articles.content: Goldman Sachs Group Inc. named Akila Raman as global head of its private and alternatives capital markets business, according to a memo seen by Bloomberg News.

articles.title: Wall Street’s Bullish Stocks View Contrasts With Consumer Gloom
articles.content: Stocks are trading near a record high, signaling Wall Street is learning to cope with lingering geopolitical risks. Main Street is struggling to catch up.

articles.title: Russia to suspend Kazakh oil flows through key pipeline supplying Berlin
articles.content: Plan will hit Rosneft refinery that supplies most of German capital’s petrol, kerosene and heating fuel

articles.title: Boom de compras al exterior: importaciones vía courier crecieron 123% en marzo y marcaron récord histórico
articles.content: Los envíos puerta a puerta alcanzaron u$s103 millones y registraron el segundo mayor nivel mensual de la serie. Además del dólar, hubo una mejora relativa del salario medido en moneda dura impulsan el consumo online de productos importados.

articles.title: Mundial 2026: cuánto cuesta reunirse para ver un partido de la Selección argentina
articles.content: Un informe de Worldpanel by Numerator analiza cómo se organizan los argentinos para ver los partidos. Desde dónde se reúnen, hasta qué consumen y qué tan fuerte es el interés por la Selección.

articles.title: Federal Reserve Board issues enforcement action with former employee of First Financial Bank
articles.content: Federal Reserve Board issues enforcement action with former employee of First Financial Bank

articles.title: TD Bank Mulls Hedging Data Center Debt With Rare SRT Deal
articles.content: Toronto-Dominion Bank is weighing a rare type of significant risk transfer to hedge current and future data center exposure as tech companies ramp up artificial intelligence investment.

articles.title: Toronto Exchange Owner to Buy Cboe’s Australia, Canada Units
articles.content: Toronto Stock Exchange owner TMX Group Ltd. is set to acquire Cboe Global Markets Inc.’s Australian and Canadian units.

articles.title: A Private Equity Reset
articles.content: Private equity is entering a new era according to Mark Sotir, the President of  Equity Group Investments, He joins Bloomberg Open Interest to break down why founders are rushing for stability, how AI is boosting productivity, not replacing jobs, and why rising interest rates and competition are making it harder than ever to generate returns. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: UBS Faces $20 Billion Capital Need Despite Swiss Concessions
articles.content: The Swiss government outlined plans that would require UBS Group AG to add $20 billion to its capital buffers, even as it waters down some planned changes to regulations set to hit the nation’s largest bank.

articles.title: Avis Surge Crushes Short Sellers
articles.content: Avis shares tripled in two weeks, crushing short sellers with $2.5 billion in losses. But bears are doubling down—short interest now near a decade high at 58%. Analysts question the rally’s fundamentals, warning the surge looks like a short squeeze, not real growth. Bloomberg's Jordan Fitzgerald joined Bloomberg Open Interest with details. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: S&P 500 Set for Record High on Earnings; Oil Gains: Markets Wrap
articles.content: A slew of strong corporate profits lifted stocks a day after President Donald Trump extended a ceasefire with Iran, though attempts to resolve the conflict struggled without any signs that the key Strait of Hormuz will reopen soon. Oil climbed.

articles.title: Hormuz Traffic at Near-Halt as Ships Come Under Gunfire
articles.content: The Strait of Hormuz stayed at a near standstill Wednesday as Iranian forces opened fire on — and seized — commercial ships.

articles.title: Petrobras Board Member Seeks Fuel Price Increase to Stem Losses
articles.content: A Petrobras board member appointed by non-controlling shareholders is warning that the state-controlled producer is losing billions from below-market fuel sales and risks reputational damage.

articles.title: Switzerland hits UBS with proposed $20bn capital increase
articles.content: Federal Council sets out plans for banking reform after months of lobbying by country’s biggest lender

articles.title: El índice líder mejoró pero aún hay un 85% de chances que en los próximos meses la economía entre en recesión
articles.content: El Índice Líder (IL) crece 0,83% en su versión desestacionalizada durante marzo pero en términos interanuales, el Índice Líder decrece 4,93% con respecto a febrero de 2025.

articles.title: GE Vernova Soars as Quarterly Orders Topped All of Last Year
articles.content: GE Vernova Inc., one of the world’s only makers of big natural-gas turbines, soared the most in intraday trading since mid-December after its electrification unit sold more grid equipment, including substations and transformers, to data center customers in the first quarter than all of last year.

articles.title: Bessent Says US Allies in Gulf, Asia Requested Swap Lines
articles.content: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said Wednesday that many Persian Gulf allies along with a number of Asian nations have requested foreign exchange swap lines with the US, and pointed to the potential for such arrangements to support dollar-denominated lending overseas.

articles.title: Oil Servicer Weatherford Sees War Impacts Deepening This Quarter
articles.content: Weatherford International Plc., one of the world’s largest oilfield-service providers, is warning that a hit to earnings in the wake of the Iran war will get worse this quarter, before an eventual rebound.

articles.title: Emerging Currencies Waver as Conflict Drags On, Oil Prices Rise
articles.content: Emerging-market currencies faltered Wednesday as money managers waded through the latest raft of headlines out of the Middle East, weighing President Donald Trump’s truce extension against Iran’s attacks on ships passing through the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: S&P 500 Hovers Near Its All-Time High; Oil Climbs: Markets Wrap
articles.content: A slew of strong corporate profits lifted stocks a day after President Donald Trump extended a ceasefire with Iran, though attempts to resolve the conflict struggled without any signs that the key Strait of Hormuz will reopen soon. Oil climbed.

articles.title: United CEO Sidesteps Merger Topic, Says Assets of Interest
articles.content: United Airlines Holdings Inc. Chief Executive Officer Scott Kirby sidestepped questions about a potential merger with American Airlines Group Inc. while acknowledging that the current climate of high costs and struggling rivals might offer opportunities for deals.

articles.title: S&P Global Vice Chairman: Hormuz Crisis Is Driving Global Change
articles.content:
Consumers are already feeling the effects of the closure of the Strait of Hormuz with energy prices surging around the world. But what if the crisis is signaling a longer-term global power shift?

On this episode of the Odd Lots podcast, S&P Global Vice Chairman Daniel Yergin tells Joe Weisenthal and Tracy Alloway that a "different world" will emerge, regardless of the war's ultimate outcome. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Blackstone Credit Fund Looks to Raise at Least $500 Million
articles.content: Blackstone Private Credit Fund is seeking to raise at least $500 million from an investment-grade note sale, people with direct knowledge of the matter said, as private lenders known as business development companies ramp up debt issuance after a dry spell.

articles.title: How Trump is bolstering his Gulf force
articles.content: A third US carrier group and up to 10,000 elite troops expected in region by end of month

articles.title: El dólar oficial sube y la brecha con el CCL supera el 6%
articles.content: El mercado sigue favorecido por la liquidación del agro, emisiones privadas y menor demanda para atesoramiento.

articles.title: ECB’s Cipollone Not Worried About Delay of Digital Euro Vote
articles.content: European Central Bank Executive Board Piero Cipollone isn’t too worried about a potential delay of a key vote in the European Parliament on the digital euro project from early May to mid-June.

articles.title: DOJ Expected to Ease Marijuana Restrictions as Soon as Wednesday
articles.content: The US Justice Department is expected to reclassify marijuana into a less restrictive federal category as soon as Wednesday, according to a person familiar with the matter.

articles.title: BRB Shareholders Greenlight Capital Raise Up to $1.8 Billion
articles.content: Banco de Brasilia SA’s shareholders approved a capital raise of as much as 8.8 billion reais ($1.8 billion) to help bolster the Brazilian bank after it was stung by transactions it made with the failed Banco Master SA.

articles.title: Ivory Coast Eyes Quarterly Price Reviews Amid Global Cocoa Crash
articles.content: Ivory Coast is considering quarterly reviews of its domestic cocoa price as it looks to adjust to the huge slump in the international market and keep its beans competitive.

articles.title: Trump Media CEO’s Exit Caps a 90% Drop as MAGA Stocks Stumble
articles.content: Truth Social has rarely been more important on Wall Street, where traders hang on White House posts that can send the market tumbling or soaring back up again. The company’s stock, though, is much less in vogue.

articles.title: Convenience Store Chain Yesway Climbs 10% After $280 Million IPO
articles.content: Yesway Inc. shares jumped 10% in the company’s trading debut, after the convenience store operator raised $280 million in an upsized initial public offering.

articles.title: Europe Diversifies Jet Fuel Supply But Import Pressure Is Rising
articles.content: Europe will need to find abundant new supplies of jet fuel if it’s to maintain its usual level of imports over the summer holiday season.

articles.title: Rogers Hits Estimates, Boosts Cash Outlook on Spending Cut
articles.content: Rogers Communications Inc. matched analysts’ earnings estimates and raised its 2026 outlook for free cash flow as it cuts capital spending. The shares rose.

articles.title: Death of US Agents in Mexico Raises Fresh Sovereignty Concerns
articles.content: The Mexican government said it was unaware that two US officials took part in an operation to dismantle a drug lab in northern Mexico, a development that threatens to escalate tensions with Washington over security and sovereignty.

articles.title: Energy traders dig in for a long Iran war
articles.content: Vitol, Trafigura and rivals expand credit lines in anticipation of prolonged disruption to global oil and gas flows

articles.title: AI should not drive today’s interest rate decisions
articles.content: How the technology will affect prices is still uncertain

articles.title: AI robot outplays humans in table tennis milestone
articles.content: Sony’s ‘Ace’ defeats elite players, highlighting how AI is improving machines’ abilities to interact with people

articles.title: DOJ Expected to Ease Marijuana Controls as Soon as Wednesday
articles.content: The US Justice Department is expected to reclassify marijuana into a less restrictive federal category as soon as Wednesday, according to a person familiar with the matter.

articles.title: Leftist Candidate in Copper Giant Peru Wants New Mining Rules
articles.content: A leftist presidential candidate on the verge of reaching Peru’s runoff election is pledging to overhaul mining rules in one of the world’s leading copper-exporting countries.

articles.title: Trump’s Shopping Spree for Equity Stakes Nears $21 Billion
articles.content: The Trump administration’s $20.9 billion buying spree for equity stakes in private-sector companies is the biggest such push into strategic industries since the Second World War, according to research by the Council on Foreign Relations.

articles.title: UBS Faces $20 Billion Hike in Capital Needs as Swiss Split Grows
articles.content: The Swiss government outlined plans that would add billions of dollars to UBS Group AG’s capital requirements, deepening a showdown with the lender over its proposed banking reforms.

articles.content: A slew of strong corporate profits lifted stocks a day after President Donald Trump extended a ceasefire with Iran, though attempts to resolve the conflict struggled without any signs that the key Strait of Hormuz will reopen soon. Oil gained.

articles.title: Brazil Oil Driller Expanding in Venezuela as US Eases Sanctions
articles.content: A Brazilian oil company is expanding in Venezuela in a bet that sanctions relief will stimulate the country’s battered industry, highlighting regional appetite for participating in the US-led revival.

articles.title: Blackstone Credit Fund Set to Raise $850 Million From Bond Sale
articles.content: Blackstone Private Credit Fund has launched an $850 million investment-grade bond sale as private lenders known as business development companies ramp up debt issuance after a dry spell.

articles.content: Este tipo de remodelaciones no se trata solamente de cambios de muebles, implica gastos de construcción.

articles.title: 'Early Innings' of US Rare-Earth Independence: Humpton
articles.content: Barbara Humpton, CEO of USA Rare Earth, joins Dani Burger on "Bloomberg Deals." USA Rare Earth agreed to acquire Brazil’s Serra Verde Group in a $2.8 billion cash-and-stock transaction, marking one of the largest deals in the rare-earths industry. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Centerview’s Tony Kim Says AI Changes Pace, Structure of Deals
articles.content: The pace of advances in artificial intelligence is changing the structure of how deals are getting done in the space, according to Tony Kim, Centerview Partners’ co-president of investment banking.

articles.title: S&P Near Peak as Chipmakers Rise 16th Straight Day: Markets Wrap
articles.content: Stocks hovered near their all-time highs as a slew of strong earnings reports and President Donald Trump’s extension of a ceasefire with Iran revived risk appetites after a two-day retreat. Bitcoin rallied.

articles.title: USA Rare Earth CEO Says US is in ‘Early Innings’ of Independence
articles.content: USA Rare Earth Inc.’s $2.8 billion acquisition of Brazil’s Serra Verde Group is a step toward US independence from China for rare earth metals, said Chief Executive Officer Barbara Humpton.

articles.title: Texas Stock Exchange Expects First IPO in Early 2027
articles.content: Jeb Hensarling, the former US representative who is now an adviser to the Texas Stock Exchange, says the first initial public offerings on the startup bourse may come in 2027's first quarter. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Traders Return to Fed Funds Wager That Surged During Repo Stress
articles.content: Traders notched up record volumes in one narrow corner of the US interest-rate futures market this week, betting on a spread that reflects potential swings in overnight lending markets.

articles.title: Trump Pushes $500 Million Plan to Save Spirit Airlines
articles.content: The Trump administration is nearing a rescue package for Spirit Airlines that could give the US government the option to own as much as 90% of the carrier once it emerges from bankruptcy. Bloomberg Intelligence Senior Aerospace Analyst George Ferguson discusses on Bloomberg Radio. (Corrects spelling of Spirit in headline)

articles.title: US may extend $500mn in rescue funding to Spirit Airlines
articles.content: Money would come with equity warrants that could make government majority owner of struggling discount carrier

articles.content: Se trata de Roberto Frenkel, referente académico y exprofesor del Presidente. En su análisis, criticó los esquemas basados en endeudamiento externo.

articles.title: Reforma tributaria: Neuquén avisa que puede recibir IVA y bajar la alícuota total
articles.content: El gobernador Rolando Figueroa dijo que los neuquinos están a la altura del desafío. La Casa Rosada tiene que presentar este año un proyecto de ley al Congreso según lo acordado con el FMI.

articles.content: US stocks headed for a fresh all-time closing high on Wednesday as President Donald Trump’s ceasefire extension with Iran boosted optimism among traders.

articles.content: KKR & Co. is investing $1.5 billion in Vertical Bridge REIT LLC in a deal that values the tower operator at $10 billion to $15 billion, according to people familiar with the matter.

articles.content: Jay Leno’s latest hosting gig involves a classic car, an airport construction site and municipal bonds.

articles.content: Shares of Fair Isaac Corp., known as FICO, sank on Wednesday, after the US government took steps that officials said were aimed at cutting costs for credit scores and making homeownership accessible to a wider swath of buyers.

The Trump administration is nearing a rescue package for Spirit Airlines that could give the US government the option to own as much as 90% of the carrier once it emerges from bankruptcy, according to people familiar with the matter.  (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Activists Want to Avoid 'Expensive' Proxy Fights: Mehrotra
articles.content:
Avinash Mehrotra, co-head of Americas M&A and global head of activism & takeover defense at Goldman Sachs, discusses the current landscape of activist landscape investing with Dani Burger on "Bloomberg Deals."
 (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: BofA's Theisen: Markets Adapting Well to Volatility
articles.content: Bank of America Global Head of FICC E-Trading and Markets Strategic Investments Sonali Theisen discusses the key factors currently driving market activity and how ETFs have 'created a language' in fixed income. She talks with Katie Greifeld and Romaine Bostick on "The Close." (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Emerging FX Sinks as Iran War Drags On, Oil Holds Over $100
articles.content: Most emerging-market currencies weakened on Wednesday as traders weighed US President Donald Trump’s truce extension against Iran’s attacks on ships passing through the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: The World Will Never Forget the Hormuz Crisis
articles.content: Chokepoints are here to stay.

articles.title: Tesla’s Earnings Beat Expectations Amid Focus on Robotics, Driverless Cars
articles.content:
Bloomberg's Steve Man breaks down Tesla earnings that beat expectations, sending the EV maker's stock up in the aftermarket. Tesla earnings. 
The positive report comes as Tesla has been trying to ramp up sluggish sales in the US and Europe and as well as new production, turning its focus to robotics and self driving cars. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Tesla 1Q Earnings Beat Estimates Sending Share Higher
articles.content: Ed Ludlow gives us a first read on Tesla's first-quarter earning which beat Wall Street estimates. Adjusted earnings per share were 41 cents beating the estimate of 34 cents. Share rose sharply after regular trading in New York. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: S&P 500 Climbs to All-Time Highs; Tesla Jumps Late: Markets Wrap

articles.content: Jonathan Lewinsohn, managing partner and co-founder of Diameter Capital, joins Dani Burger on "Bloomberg Deals." Diameter Capital sees a “reckoning threatening” private credit amid the recent investor exodus spurred by a software selloff, according to Jonathan Lewinsohn, managing partner and co-founder of Diameter Capital Partners. articles.title: Suburban Propane CEO: Producing Record Levels Domestically
articles.content: Suburban Propane President and CEO Michael Stivala joins Bloomberg Businessweek Daily to discuss the company's current supply and demand outlook as the Iran war weighs on commodities and consumer costs across the board. Stivala says the company's robust domestic supply is keeping inventory levels elevated at a record high.  He speaks with Carol Massar and Tim Stenovec. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Asian Equities to Rise After US Stocks Hit Record: Markets Wrap
articles.content: Shares in Asia were primed for advances Thursday after robust US corporate results and an extension to the US-Iran ceasefire lifted Wall Street to a record.

articles.title: Investors Adjust to New US-Iran Normal: No Peace, No War
articles.content: In another reversal, US President Donald Trump announced a unilateral, open-ended extension of the ceasefire in the Iran war. Now, there’s no peace, but no active war either. This may become the new normal: a fragile ceasefire without a formal deal, with tensions simmering, sporadic flare-ups, and oil near $100. A geo-economics analysis by Bloomberg Economics highlights that the war may be settling into a prolonged middle ground: on the one hand, no formal end to the hostilities between the US/Israel and Iran because the underlying differences remain unresolved, and on the other hand, a costly escalation that draws the US deeper into a conflict it doesn't want. Ray Takeyh, Council on Foreign Relations Hasib J. Sabbagh Senior Fellow for Middle East Studies, joins Bloomberg Businessweek Daily to discuss. He speaks with Carol Massar and Tim Stenovec. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Oil Holds Gains as Peace Negotiations Between US and Iran Stall
articles.content: Oil held a three-day gain as the US and Iran remained locked in a battle for control of the Strait of Hormuz after failing to meet for a fresh round of peace talks.

articles.title: Tesla boosts spending forecast to $25bn as Musk doubles down on AI bet
articles.content: CEO warns investors to expect ‘very significant’ spending increase on self-driving taxis, trucks, robots and chip factories

articles.title: Trump envoy seeks to replace Iran with Italy in football World Cup
articles.content: Request to Fifa is pitched as effort to repair US ties with country even as Iran says it is ready to participate

articles.title: Iran war drives Panama Canal lane prices to record high
articles.content: Asian buyers seeking western crude push bids five times higher than pre-conflict levels

articles.title: Tesla Boosts Spending Plan in AI, Robotics Push
articles.content: Tesla anticipates billions of dollars in additional spending this year to support Elon Musk’s ambitions to transform the electric-vehicle pioneer into an AI and robotics company. Bloomberg's Dana Hull reports on how investors are reacting. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Philippine Rate Decision Too Close to Call on Stagflation Risk
articles.content: The Iran war’s fallout has made the Philippine central bank’s next policy move a tough call: tighten to tame price pressures from the energy crunch, or stand pat to support a fragile economy.

articles.title: Why It’s Getting Hard to Use Miles to Book Your Flight
articles.content: Air miles are becoming a precious commodity as uncertainty grows about the outlook for ticket prices and the availability of flights.

articles.title: Tesla boosts spending plans to $25bn as Musk doubles down on AI bet
articles.content: CEO warns investors to expect ‘very significant’ spending increase on self-driving taxis, trucks, robots and chip factories

articles.title: La economía se hundió 2,6% mensual en febrero, su peor retroceso desde diciembre de 2023
articles.content: La actividad económica se vio afectada por el mal desempeño de sectores clave, como la industria y el comercio, y el fin de la cosecha de trigo. En términos anuales retrocedió 2,1%.

articles.title: Stocks Fluctuate at Open, Oil Stays Above $100: Markets Wrap
articles.content: Stocks and crude oil swung at the open in a nervous start to trading in Asia, as lingering concerns about the war in Iran clashed with strong corporate earnings that are helping steady investor sentiment.

articles.title: Goldman's Calnon on Market Outlook Amid Hormuz Closure
articles.content: Global Head of Public Investing at Goldman Sachs Asset Management Greg Calnon talks to Bloomberg TV about the resilience of global markets despite an extended closure of the Strait of Hormuz, and suggests that investors are still pricing in a relatively speedy restoration of calm to energy supplies. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Oil Extends Gain as Peace Negotiations Between US and Iran Stall
articles.content: Oil rose for a fourth day as the US and Iran remained locked in a battle for control of the Strait of Hormuz after failing to meet for a fresh round of peace talks.

articles.title: NZ Finance Minister Says Economic Recovery Delayed, Not Derailed
articles.content: New Zealand’s nascent economic recovery has been set back by the war in Iran, but is still expected to get back on track later this year, Finance Minister Nicola Willis said.

articles.title: Australia’s Sovereign Wealth Fund Names Brandweiner as CIO
articles.content: Australia’s sovereign wealth fund, the A$335 billion ($240 billion) Future Fund, named Richard Brandweiner as chief investment officer, ending a months-long search to fill the role.

articles.title: Garuda Leads Losses in Bonds of Asian Airlines Stung by Iran War
articles.content: Bonds of Asian airlines, led by PT Garuda Indonesia, are showing strain from the Iran war, as higher fuel burdens compared with global peers amplify their vulnerabilities.

articles.title: Intel boosted as Musk says his Terafab will use its latest chipmaking tech
articles.content: US semiconductor group has been seeking a big customer for its ‘14A’ manufacturing system

articles.title: US allies in Gulf and Asia have requested swap lines, Bessent says
articles.content: Treasury secretary says ‘numerous’ countries sought support amid economic fallout from Iran war

articles.title: US Navy secretary fired amid Iran blockade
articles.content: John Phelan ousted after clashing with defence secretary Pete Hegseth

articles.title: Tesla Boosts Spending Plan to $25 Billion in AI, Robotics Push
articles.content: Tesla anticipates billions of dollars in additional spending this year to support Elon Musk’s ambitions to transform the electric-vehicle pioneer into an AI and robotics company. Ed Ludlow reports. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: NZ Finance Minister Says Recovery Delayed, Not Derailed
articles.content: New Zealand’s nascent economic recovery has been impacted by the war in Iran, but is still expected to get back on track later this year, Finance Minister Nicola Willis said.

articles.title: Indonesia Sells ¥172.1 Billion Samurai Bond on Strong Demand
articles.content: Indonesia raised ¥172.1 billion ($1.1 billion) in its biggest Samurai bond sale in two years as strong investor demand for its credit outweighed concerns about budgetary pressures due to the Middle East war.

articles.title: Counterpoint: SK Hynix is Riding the Supercycle
articles.content: Tom Kang, Research Director at Counterpoint, discusses the outlook for SK Hynix as the company reported a five-fold jump in quarterly profit and reiterated plans to ratchet up its capex this year. He speaks with Haidi Stroud-Watts and Paul Allen on Bloomberg: The Asia Trade. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: GSAM's Calnon on Markets, Investment Strategy
articles.content: Greg Calnon, Global Head of Public Investing at Goldman Sachs Asset Management, discusses his outlook for markets and investment strategy. He speaks with Haidi Stroud-Watts and Paul Allen on Bloomberg: The Asia Trade.  (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Alibaba-Backed Zelos Is Said to Plan Hong Kong IPO to Raise About $600 Million
articles.content: Chinese robovan operator Zelos Technology is planning an initial public offering in Hong Kong that could raise about $600 million, according to people familiar with the matter.

articles.title: Modi Stakes Prestige on Key State Poll as Voter Roll Row Grows
articles.content: Just two weeks before voters in India’s eastern state of West Bengal were due to go to the polls, about 9 million names were removed from the registered roll, causing an uproar among opposition groups.

articles.title: Dollar Use in Global Trade Rose to High Amid War, Swift Says
articles.content: The ongoing conflict in the Middle East is solidifying the US dollar’s dominant role in global trade, according to one measure of activity in the interbank foreign-exchange markets.

articles.title: Intel lifted as Musk says his Terafab will use its latest chipmaking tech
articles.content: US semiconductor group has been seeking a big customer for its ‘14A’ manufacturing system
